title: TeamsChat
description: A plugin that lets operators create separate chat groups that can communicate only within themselves.
body:
# TeamsChat
**TeamsChat is a lightweight and easy-to-use Minecraft plugin that allows server operators to create and manage chat teams.** Players can be added to teams, and each team has its own chat channel isolated from the global chat.

## Commands

- **/team create <name>** - Creates a new team with the specified name

- **/team delete <name>** - Deletes the specified team and removes all its members

- **/team add <team> <player>** - Adds a player to the given team

- **/team remove <team> <player>** - Removes a player from the given team

- **/team list** - Displays all existing teams

- **/team info <player>** - Shows which team a specific player belongs to

- **/team-chat <on|off>** - Enables or disables team chat plugin

**Important:** A player who is not assigned to a group by the operators will not be able to send messages and will instead receive the automatic message: "you don’t have a team so you can’t chat."

## Staff Team

By default, a group named "Staff" is automatically created. Adding a player to this group will allow them to see all messages on the server, and any message they send will be broadcast and visible to all players.

## Self-Join to Teams

The plugin includes a mode that when enabled, allows players to freely join teams without the need for staff involvement.
This mode can be toggled in the config:


```
allow-player-join: false/true (default is false)
```

or by using the command:

- /team allow-join <on|off>
